info.title: Intercom API (curated subset)
info.description: Curated subset of the real, published Atlassian spec (source: intercom.json), trimmed to the operations Anvil backtests against a mature reference MCP server.

put.description: You can update an existing contact (ie. user or lead).

post.description:
You can search for multiple contacts by the value of their attributes in order to fetch exactly who you want. To search for contacts, you need to send a `POST` request to `https://api.intercom.io/contacts/search`. This will accept a query object in the body which will define your filters in order to search for contacts. {% admonition type="warning" name="Optimizing search queries" %} Search queries can be complex, so optimizing them can help the performance of your search. Use the `AND` and `OR` operators to combine multiple filters to get the exact results you need and utilize pagination to limit the number of results returned. The default is `50` results per page. See the [pagination section](https://developers.intercom.com/docs/build-an-integration/learn-more/rest-apis/pagination/#example-search-conversations-request) for more details on how to use the `starting_after` param. {% /admonition %} ### Merged Contacts Contacts that have been merged (via POST /contacts/merge) are excluded from search results. If a contact was recently merged into another, it will no longer appear in queries filtered by `updated_at` or any other field. Only the target contact from the merge remains searchable. ### Contact Creation Delay If a contact has recently been created, there is a possibility that it will not yet be available when searching. This means that it may not appear in the response. This delay can take a few minutes. If you need to be instantly notified it is recommended to use webhooks and iterate to see if they match your search filters. ### Nesting & Limitations You can nest these filters in order to get even more granular insights that pinpoint exactly what you need. Example: (1 OR 2) AND (3 OR 4). There are some limitations to the amount of multiple's there can be: * There's a limit of max 2 nested filters * There's a limit of max 15 filters for each AND or OR group ### Searching for Timestamp Fields All timestamp fields (created_at, updated_at etc.) are indexed as Dates for Contact Search queries; Datetime queries are not currently supported. This means you can only query for timestamp fields by day - not hour, minute or second. The day a timestamp falls on is determined using your workspace's timezone, so the same query can return different results across workspaces in different timezones. Because timestamps are stored in UTC, filtering by a value the API returned may not match the originating contact when your workspace is not set to UTC. For example, on a workspace set to UTC, if you search for all Contacts with a created_at value greater (>) than 1577869200 (the UNIX timestamp for January 1st, 2020 9:00 AM UTC), that will be interpreted as 1577836800 (January 1st, 2020 12:00 AM UTC). The search results will then include Contacts created from January 2nd, 2020 12:00 AM UTC onwards. On a workspace in another timezone, the day boundaries fall on that timezone's midnight instead. If you'd like to get contacts created on January 1st, 2020 you should search with a created_at value equal (=) to 1577836800 (January 1st, 2020 12:00 AM UTC). This behaviour applies only to timestamps used in search queries. The search results will still contain the full UNIX timestamp and be sorted accordingly. ### Accepted Fields Most key listed as part of the Contacts Model are searchable, whether writeable or not. The value you search for has to match the accepted type, otherwise the query will fail (ie. as `created_at` accepts a date, the `value` cannot be a string such as `"foorbar"`). | Field | Type | | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | | id | String | | role | String
Accepts user or lead | | name | String | | avatar | String | | owner_id | Integer | | email | String | | email_domain | String | | phone | String | | external_id | String | | created_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | signed_up_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | updated_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | last_seen_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | last_contacted_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | last_replied_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | last_email_opened_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | last_email_clicked_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | language_override | String | | browser | String | | browser_language | String | | os | String | | location.country | String | | location.region | String | | location.city | String | | unsubscribed_from_emails | Boolean | | marked_email_as_spam | Boolean | | has_hard_bounced | Boolean | | ios_last_seen_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | ios_app_version | String | | ios_device | String | | ios_app_device | String | | ios_os_version | String | | ios_app_name | String | | ios_sdk_version | String | | android_last_seen_at | Date (UNIX Timestamp) | | android_app_version | String | | android_device | String | | android_app_name | String | | andoid_sdk_version | String | | segment_id | String | | tag_id | String | | custom_attributes.{attribute_name} | String | ### Accepted Operators {% admonition type="warning" name="Searching based on `created_at`" %} You cannot use the `<=` or `>=` operators to search by `created_at`. {% /admonition %} The table below shows the operators you can use to define how you want to search for the value. The operator should be put in as a string (`"="`). The operator has to be compatible with the field's type (eg. you cannot search with `>` for a given string value as it's only compatible for integer's and dates). | Operator | Valid Types | Description | | :------- | :------------------------------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------- | | = | All | Equals | | != | All | Doesn't Equal | | IN | All | In
Shortcut for `OR` queries
Values must be in Array | | NIN | All | Not In
Shortcut for `OR !` queries
Values must be in Array | | > | Integer
Date (UNIX Timestamp) | Greater than | | < | Integer
Date (UNIX Timestamp) | Lower than | | ~ | String | Contains | | !~ | String | Doesn't Contain | | ^ | String | Starts With | | $ | String | Ends With |

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of results to fetch. Defaults to first page

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: How many results to display per page. Defaults to 15

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: String used to get the next page of conversations.

post.description: You can create a new contact (ie. user or lead).

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: String used to get the next page of conversations.

post.description:
You can create a conversation that has been initiated by a contact (ie. user or lead).
The conversation can be an in-app message only.

{% admonition type="info" name="Sending for visitors" %}
You can also send a message from a visitor by specifying their `user_id` or `id` value in the `from` field, along with a `type` field value of `contact`.
This visitor will be automatically converted to a contact with a lead role once the conversation is created.
{% /admonition %}

This will return the Message model that has been created.

parameters.name: conversation_id
parameters.description: The id of the conversation to target

parameters.name: display_as
parameters.description: Set to plaintext to retrieve conversation messages in plain text.

parameters.name: conversation_id
parameters.description: The Intercom provisioned identifier for the conversation or the string "last" to reply to the last part of the conversation

intercom_version.description: Intercom API version.</br>By default, it's equal to the version set in the app package.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Contact are the objects that represent your leads and users in Intercom.

id.description: The unique identifier for the contact which is given by Intercom.

external_id.description: The unique identifier for the contact which is provided by the Client.

workspace_id.description: The id of the workspace which the contact belongs to.

formatted_phone.description: The contacts phone number normalized to the E164 format

owner_id.description: The id of an admin that has been assigned account ownership of the contact.

has_hard_bounced.description: Whether the contact has had an email sent to them hard bounce.

marked_email_as_spam.description: Whether the contact has marked an email sent to them as spam.

unsubscribed_from_emails.description: Whether the contact is unsubscribed from emails.

created_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time when the contact was created.

updated_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time when the contact was last updated.

signed_up_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time specified for when a contact signed up.

last_seen_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time when the contact was last seen (either where the Intercom Messenger was installed or when specified manually).

last_replied_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time when the contact last messaged in.

last_contacted_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time when the contact was last messaged.

last_email_opened_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time when the contact last opened an email.

last_email_clicked_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time when the contact last clicked a link in an email.

language_override.description: A preferred language setting for the contact, used by the Intercom Messenger even if their browser settings change.

browser.description: The name of the browser which the contact is using.

browser_version.description: The version of the browser which the contact is using.

browser_language.description: The language set by the browser which the contact is using.

os.description: The operating system which the contact is using.

android_app_name.description: The name of the Android app which the contact is using.

android_app_version.description: The version of the Android app which the contact is using.

android_device.description: The Android device which the contact is using.

android_os_version.description: The version of the Android OS which the contact is using.

android_sdk_version.description: The version of the Android SDK which the contact is using.

android_last_seen_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time when the contact was last seen on an Android device.

ios_app_name.description: The name of the iOS app which the contact is using.

ios_app_version.description: The version of the iOS app which the contact is using.

ios_device.description: The iOS device which the contact is using.

ios_os_version.description: The version of iOS which the contact is using.

ios_sdk_version.description: The version of the iOS SDK which the contact is using.

ios_last_seen_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The last time the contact used the iOS app.

custom_attributes.description: The custom attributes which are set for the contact.

image_url.description: An image URL containing the avatar of a contact.

error.title: Error
error.description: The API will return an Error List for a failed request, which will contain one or more Error objects.

errors.description: An array of one or more error objects

code.description: A string indicating the kind of error, used to further qualify the HTTP response code

message.description: Optional. Human readable description of the error.

field.description: Optional. Used to identify a particular field or query parameter that was in error.

external_id.description: A unique identifier for the contact which is given to Intercom

avatar.description: An image URL containing the avatar of a contact

signed_up_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time specified for when a contact signed up.

last_seen_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time when the contact was last seen (either where the Intercom Messenger was installed or when specified manually).

owner_id.description: The id of an admin that has been assigned account ownership of the contact

unsubscribed_from_emails.description: Whether the contact is unsubscribed from emails

custom_attributes.description: The custom attributes which are set for the contact

contact_list.description: Contacts are your users in Intercom.

external_id.description: A unique identifier for the contact which is given to Intercom

avatar.description: An image URL containing the avatar of a contact

signed_up_at.description: (Unix timestamp in seconds) The time specified for when a contact signed up.

paginated_conversation_response.title: Conversation List
paginated_conversation_response.description: Conversations are how you can communicate with users in Intercom. They are created when a contact replies to an outbound message, or when one admin directly sends a message to a single contact.

total_count.description: A count of the total number of objects.

message.title: Message
message.description: Message are how you reach out to contacts in Intercom. They are created when an admin sends an outbound message to a contact.

created_at.description: The time the conversation was created.

subject.description: The subject of the message. Only present if message_type: email.

body.description: The message body, which may contain HTML.

message_type.description: The type of message that was sent. Can be email, inapp, facebook or twitter.

create_conversation_request.description: Conversations are how you can communicate with users in Intercom. They are created when a contact replies to an outbound message, or when one admin directly sends a message to a single contact.
create_conversation_request.title: Create Conversation Request Payload

type.description: The role associated to the contact - user or lead.

id.description: The identifier for the contact which is given by Intercom.

body.description: The content of the message. HTML is not supported.

subject.description: The title of the email. Only applicable if the message type is email.

attachment_urls.description: A list of image URLs that will be added as attachments. You can include up to 10 URLs.

created_at.description: The time the conversation was created as a UTC Unix timestamp. If not provided, the current time will be used. This field is only recommneded for migrating past conversations from another source into Intercom.

conversation.title: Conversation
conversation.description: Conversations are how you can communicate with users in Intercom. They are created when a contact replies to an outbound message, or when one admin directly sends a message to a single contact.

title.description: The title given to the conversation.

created_at.description: The time the conversation was created.

updated_at.description: The last time the conversation was updated.

waiting_since.description: The last time a Contact responded to an Admin. In other words, the time a customer started waiting for a response. Set to null if last reply is from an Admin.

snoozed_until.description: If set this is the time in the future when this conversation will be marked as open. i.e. it will be in a snoozed state until this time. i.e. it will be in a snoozed state until this time.

open.description: Indicates whether a conversation is open (true) or closed (false).

read.description: Indicates whether a conversation has been read.

priority.description: If marked as priority, it will return priority or else not_priority.

admin_assignee_id.description: The id of the admin assigned to the conversation. If it's not assigned to an admin it will return null.

team_assignee_id.description: The id of the team assigned to the conversation. If it's not assigned to a team it will return null.

ai_agent_participated.description: Indicates whether the AI Agent participated in the conversation.

contact_tags.title: Contact Tags
contact_tags.description: An object containing tags meta data about the tags that a contact has. Up to 10 will be displayed here. Use the url to get more.

data.description: This object represents the tags attached to a contact.

url.description: url to get more tag resources for this contact

total_count.description: Int representing the total number of tags attached to this contact

has_more.description: Whether there's more Addressable Objects to be viewed. If true, use the url to view all

contact_notes.title: Contact notes
contact_notes.description: An object containing notes meta data about the notes that a contact has. Up to 10 will be displayed here. Use the url to get more.

data.description: This object represents the notes attached to a contact.

url.description: Url to get more company resources for this contact

total_count.description: Int representing the total number of companyies attached to this contact

has_more.description: Whether there's more Addressable Objects to be viewed. If true, use the url to view all

contact_companies.title: Contact companies
contact_companies.description: An object containing companies meta data about the companies that a contact has. Up to 10 will be displayed here. Use the url to get more.

url.description: Url to get more company resources for this contact

total_count.description: Int representing the total number of companyies attached to this contact

has_more.description: Whether there's more Addressable Objects to be viewed. If true, use the url to view all

contact_location.title: Contact Location
contact_location.description: An object containing location meta data about a Intercom contact.

country.description: The country that the contact is located in

region.description: The overal region that the contact is located in

city.description: The city that the contact is located in

contact_social_profiles.title: Social Profile
contact_social_profiles.description: An object containing social profiles that a contact has.

data.description: A list of social profiles objects associated with the contact.

cursor_pages.title: Cursor based pages
cursor_pages.description:
Cursor-based pagination is a technique used in the Intercom API to navigate through large amounts of data.
A "cursor" or pointer is used to keep track of the current position in the result set, allowing the API to return the data in small chunks or "pages" as needed.

single_filter_search_request.title: Single Filter Search Request
single_filter_search_request.description: Search using Intercoms Search APIs with a single filter.

field.description: The accepted field that you want to search on.

operator.description: The accepted operators you can use to define how you want to search for the value.

value.description: The value that you want to search on.

multiple_filter_search_request.title: Multiple Filter Search Request
multiple_filter_search_request.description: Search using Intercoms Search APIs with more than one filter.

operator.description: An operator to allow boolean inspection between multiple fields.

per_page.description: The number of results to fetch per page.

starting_after.description: The cursor to use in the next request to get the next page of results.

conversation_list_item.title: Conversation List Item
conversation_list_item.description: The data returned about your conversations when you list or search them.

title.description: The title given to the conversation.

created_at.description: The time the conversation was created.

updated_at.description: The last time the conversation was updated.

waiting_since.description: The last time a Contact responded to an Admin. In other words, the time a customer started waiting for a response. Set to null if last reply is from an Admin.

snoozed_until.description: If set this is the time in the future when this conversation will be marked as open. i.e. it will be in a snoozed state until this time. i.e. it will be in a snoozed state until this time.

open.description: Indicates whether a conversation is open (true) or closed (false).

read.description: Indicates whether a conversation has been read.

priority.description: If marked as priority, it will return priority or else not_priority.

admin_assignee_id.description: The id of the admin assigned to the conversation. If it's not assigned to an admin it will return null.

team_assignee_id.description: The id of the team assigned to the conversation. If it's not assigned to a team it will return null.

ai_agent_participated.description: Indicates whether the AI Agent participated in the conversation.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: A list of tags objects associated with a conversation
A list of tags objects associated with the conversation.

conversation_rating.title: Conversation Rating
conversation_rating.description: The Conversation Rating object which contains information on the rating and/or remark added by a Contact and the Admin assigned to the conversation.

rating.description: The rating, between 1 and 5, for the conversation.

remark.description: An optional field to add a remark to correspond to the number rating

created_at.description: The time the rating was requested in the conversation being rated.

conversation_source.title: Conversation source
conversation_source.description: The Conversation Part that originated this conversation, which can be Contact, Admin, Campaign, Automated or Operator initiated.

type.description: This includes conversation, email, facebook, instagram, phone_call, phone_switch, push, sms, twitter and whatsapp.

delivered_as.description: The conversation's initiation type. Possible values are customer_initiated, campaigns_initiated (legacy campaigns), operator_initiated (Custom bot), automated (Series and other outbounds with dynamic audience message) and admin_initiated (fixed audience message, ticket initiated by an admin, group email).

subject.description: Optional. The message subject. For Twitter, this will show a generic message regarding why the subject is obscured.

body.description: The message body, which may contain HTML. For Twitter, this will show a generic message regarding why the body is obscured.

attachments.description: A list of attachments for the part.

url.description: The URL where the conversation was started. For Twitter, Email, and Bots, this will be blank.

redacted.description: Whether or not the source message has been redacted. Only applicable for contact initiated messages.

conversation_contacts.title: Contacts
conversation_contacts.description: The list of contacts (users or leads) involved in this conversation. This will only contain one customer unless more were added via the group conversation feature.

contacts.description: The list of contacts (users or leads) involved in this conversation. This will only contain one customer unless more were added via the group conversation feature.

conversation_teammates.title: Conversation teammates
conversation_teammates.description: The list of teammates who participated in the conversation (wrote at least one conversation part).

teammates.description: The list of teammates who participated in the conversation (wrote at least one conversation part).

conversation_first_contact_reply.title: First contact reply
conversation_first_contact_reply.description: An object containing information on the first users message. For a contact initiated message this will represent the users original message.

sla_applied.title: Applied SLA
sla_applied.description:
The SLA Applied object contains the details for which SLA has been applied to this conversation.
Important: if there are any canceled sla_events for the conversation - meaning an SLA has been manually removed from a conversation, the sla_status will always be returned as null.

sla_name.description: The name of the SLA as given by the teammate when it was created.

sla_status.description:
SLA statuses:
            - `hit`: If there’s at least one hit event in the underlying sla_events table, and no “missed” or “canceled” events for the conversation.
            - `missed`: If there are any missed sla_events for the conversation and no canceled events. If there’s even a single missed sla event, the status will always be missed. A missed status is not applied when the SLA expires, only the next time a teammate replies.
            - `active`: An SLA has been applied to a conversation, but has not yet been fulfilled. SLA status is active only if there are no “hit, “missed”, or “canceled” events.

conversation_statistics.title: Conversation statistics
conversation_statistics.description: A Statistics object containing all information required for reporting, with timestamps and calculated metrics.

time_to_assignment.description: Duration until last assignment before first admin reply. In seconds.

time_to_admin_reply.description: Duration until first admin reply. Subtracts out of business hours. In seconds.

time_to_first_close.description: Duration until conversation was closed first time. Subtracts out of business hours. In seconds.

time_to_last_close.description: Duration until conversation was closed last time. Subtracts out of business hours. In seconds.

median_time_to_reply.description: Median based on all admin replies after a contact reply. Subtracts out of business hours. In seconds.

first_contact_reply_at.description: Time of first text conversation part from a contact.

first_assignment_at.description: Time of first assignment after first_contact_reply_at.

first_admin_reply_at.description: Time of first admin reply after first_contact_reply_at.

first_close_at.description: Time of first close after first_contact_reply_at.

last_assignment_at.description: Time of last assignment after first_contact_reply_at.

last_assignment_admin_reply_at.description: Time of first admin reply since most recent assignment.

last_contact_reply_at.description: Time of the last conversation part from a contact.

last_admin_reply_at.description: Time of the last conversation part from an admin.

last_close_at.description: Time of the last conversation close.

last_closed_by_id.description: The last admin who closed the conversation. Returns a reference to an Admin object.

conversation_parts.title: Conversation Parts
conversation_parts.description: A list of Conversation Part objects for each part message in the conversation. This is only returned when Retrieving a Conversation, and ignored when Listing all Conversations. There is a limit of 500 parts.

conversation_parts.title: Conversation Parts
conversation_parts.description: A list of Conversation Part objects for each part message in the conversation. This is only returned when Retrieving a Conversation, and ignored when Listing all Conversations. There is a limit of 500 parts.

linked_object_list.title: Linked Objects
linked_object_list.description: An object containing metadata about linked conversations and linked tickets. Up to 1000 can be returned.

total_count.description: The total number of linked objects.

has_more.description: Whether or not there are more linked objects than returned.

data.description: An array containing the linked conversations and linked tickets.

ai_agent.title: AI Agent
ai_agent.description: Data related to AI Agent involvement in the conversation.

source_type.description: The type of the source that triggered AI Agent involvement in the conversation.

source_title.description: The title of the source that triggered AI Agent involvement in the conversation. If this is `essentials_plan_setup` then it will return `null`.

last_answer_type.description: The type of the last answer delivered by AI Agent. If no answer was delivered then this will return `null`

resolution_state.description: The resolution state of AI Agent. If no AI or custom answer has been delivered then this will return `null`.

rating.description: The customer satisfaction rating given to AI Agent, from 1-5.

rating_remark.description: The customer satisfaction rating remark given to AI Agent.

admin_reply_conversation_request.title: Admin Reply
admin_reply_conversation_request.description: Payload of the request to reply on behalf of an admin

body.description: The text body of the reply. Notes accept some HTML formatting. Must be present for comment and note message types.

admin_id.description: The id of the admin who is authoring the comment.

created_at.description: The time the reply was created. If not provided, the current time will be used.

attachment_urls.description: A list of image URLs that will be added as attachments. You can include up to 10 URLs.

attachment_files.description: A list of files that will be added as attachments. You can include up to 10 files

addressable_list.title: Addressable List
addressable_list.description: A list used to access other resources from a parent model.

id.description: The id of the addressable object

url.description: Url to get more company resources for this contact

contact_company.title: Contact company
contact_company.description: A reference to a company associated with a contact

id.description: The unique identifier for the company

url.description: URL to get the full company resource

social_profile.title: Social Profile
social_profile.description: A Social Profile allows you to label your contacts, companies, and conversations and list them using that Social Profile.

name.description: The name of the Social media profile

url.description: The name of the Social media profile

multiple_or_single_filter_search_request.title: Multiple or Single Filter Search Request

tag.title: Tag
tag.description: A tag allows you to label your contacts, companies, and conversations and list them using that tag.

applied_at.description: The time when the tag was applied to the object. Only present when the tag is returned as part of a tagging operation on a contact, conversation, or ticket.

applied_by.description: The admin who applied the tag. Only present when the tag is returned as part of a tagging operation on a contact, conversation, or ticket.

id.description: The unique identifier for the contact which is given by Intercom.

external_id.description: The unique identifier for the contact which is provided by the Client.

conversation_part_author.title: Conversation part author
conversation_part_author.description: The object who initiated the conversation, which can be a Contact, Admin or Team. Bots and campaigns send messages on behalf of Admins or Teams. For Twitter, this will be blank.

part_attachment.title: Part attachment
part_attachment.description: The file attached to a part

content_type.description: The content type of the attachment

conversation_part.title: Conversation Part
conversation_part.description: A Conversation Part represents a message in the conversation.

id.description: The id representing the conversation part.

body.description: The message body, which may contain HTML. For Twitter, this will show a generic message regarding why the body is obscured.

created_at.description: The time the conversation part was created.

updated_at.description: The last time the conversation part was updated.

notified_at.description: The time the user was notified with the conversation part.

assigned_to.description: The id of the admin that was assigned the conversation by this conversation_part (null if there has been no change in assignment.)

attachments.title: Conversation part attachments
attachments.description: A list of attachments for the part.

external_id.description: The external id of the conversation part

redacted.description: Whether or not the conversation part has been redacted.

id.description: The ID of the linked object

category.description: Category of the Linked Ticket Object.

total_count.description: The total number of content sources used by AI Agent in the conversation.

content_sources.description: The content sources used by AI Agent in the conversation.

contact_reply_intercom_user_id_request.title: Intercom User ID
contact_reply_intercom_user_id_request.description: Payload of the request to reply on behalf of a contact using their `intercom_user_id`

intercom_user_id.description: The identifier for the contact as given by Intercom.

attachment_files.description: A list of files that will be added as attachments.

contact_reply_email_request.title: Email
contact_reply_email_request.description: Payload of the request to reply on behalf of a contact using their `email`

email.description: The email you have defined for the user.

attachment_files.description: A list of files that will be added as attachments.

contact_reply_user_id_request.title: User ID
contact_reply_user_id_request.description: Payload of the request to reply on behalf of a contact using their `user_id`

user_id.description: The external_id you have defined for the contact.

attachment_files.description: A list of files that will be added as attachments. You can include up to 10 files.

conversation_attachment_files.title: Conversation attachment files
conversation_attachment_files.description: Properties of the attachment files in a conversation part

content_type.description: The content type of the file

content_source.title: Content Source
content_source.description: The content source used by AI Agent in the conversation.

content_type.description: The type of the content source.

url.description: The internal URL linking to the content source for teammates.

title.description: The title of the content source.

locale.description: The ISO 639 language code of the content source.

body.description: The text body of the comment.

created_at.description: The time the reply was created. If not provided, the current time will be used.

attachment_urls.title: Attachment URLs
attachment_urls.description: A list of image URLs that will be added as attachments. You can include up to 10 URLs.
